title: Implement graceful shutdown
description: Wire signal handling, work draining, and a bounded deadline so deploys and scale-downs lose no in-flight work.
body: Steps: 1. Trap SIGTERM/SIGINT and flip a draining flag. 2. Stop accepting new requests/messages (close listener, pause fetch). 3. Wait for in-flight handlers with a deadline shorter than the orchestrator grace period. 4. Re-queue or nack anything unfinished; flush buffers and close pools. 5. Exit 0. Rollback point: the draining flag can be disabled to fall back to immediate exit. Validation: a SIGTERM-under-load test asserts zero dropped jobs and that readiness flips before draining so the load balancer stops routing.
